name: Check Into Cash
description_short: Check Into Cash Bakersfield, California — Check Into Cash is a 30-year-old national payday and title loan chain offering same-day cash loans up to $600 ...
description_long:
Check Into Cash was founded over 30 years ago and has grown into one of the largest payday lending chains in the United States, with stores across dozens of states including Michigan. The company positions itself as a neighborhood financial services provider, emphasizing speed, convenience, and confidentiality for customers facing short-term cash needs. Their Ferndale, Michigan location on East 9 Mile Road is one of many retail storefronts offering in-person service during extended weekday hours.

The company's core offering is short-term lending. In Michigan, their flagship product is a Payday Loan of up to $600, repaid on the customer's next pay date. Beyond payday loans, Check Into Cash offers Installment Loans with longer repayment terms and lower per-period payments, Title Loans that use a vehicle's clean title as collateral, and a Flex Line of Credit allowing repeat draws up to a set limit. Loans are available both in-store and online depending on state. Non-lending services at their Michigan locations include Check Cashing, Western Union Money Transfers, Bill Pay, Green Dot Visa Debit Cards, Tax Preparation, and Money Orders.

What distinguishes Check Into Cash from smaller operators is its three-decade track record, national retail footprint, and multi-product menu that serves both banked and underbanked customers. The application process is designed to be fast — requirements include a government-issued photo ID, Social Security number, proof of income, and an active checking account with routing number. If approved, customers can walk out the same day with cash. Online applications are also accepted for payday products in eligible states.

Check Into Cash serves a real need but comes with the same structural drawbacks as all payday lending. Rates and fees apply and are state-regulated — Michigan caps payday loans at $600, which limits their utility for larger emergencies. The requirement for an active checking account means the most financially excluded consumers may not qualify. Costs are high relative to traditional credit products. Sunday closures and limited Saturday hours reduce accessibility on weekends. Customers should review the Retail Fee Schedule (linked on the Michigan store page) before borrowing.
address: 2350 White Ln Ste B, Bakersfield, CA 93304

company_info.headquarters: 2350 White Ln Ste B, Bakersfield, CA 93304

pros: 30-year operating history — one of the most established payday loan chains nationally
Same-day in-store cash disbursement if approved
Multiple loan products: payday, installment, title loans, and flex line of credit
Non-lending services available: check cashing, Western Union, bill pay, Green Dot Visa, tax prep, money orders
Online loan applications available (payday loans) in addition to in-store
Extended weekday hours (until 7pm Mon–Fri, early 8am open on Fridays)
State license and Retail Fee Schedule publicly linked on the Michigan store page
cons: Michigan payday loans capped at $600 — insufficient for larger emergencies
Requires an active checking account, excluding fully unbanked applicants from loan products
Not all products and services are available at all locations
Sunday hours: closed; Saturday hours limited to 9am–4pm
Rates and fees are high relative to credit union alternatives or installment lenders — see state fee schedule
best_for: Employed consumers with a checking account who need up to $600 cash before their next paycheck
Customers needing check cashing or Western Union transfers without a bank relationship
Borrowers who prefer in-person service and same-day cash over online-only lenders
Michigan residents who need a regulated, licensed short-term lender with a physical storefront
diagnosis: Check Into Cash is best suited for employed, banked consumers in Michigan who need a small amount of emergency cash quickly and have exhausted lower-cost options. The main caveat is that payday loan fees are high — borrowers should read the Michigan Retail Fee Schedule before applying and treat this as a last-resort option, not a routine credit tool.
